CREATE TABLE test_strings (id INTEGER, str_value TEXT);
SELECT str_value FROM test_strings WHERE str_value ^@ 'a';
SELECT str_value FROM test_strings WHERE str_value ^@ 'b' OR str_value ^@ 'a';
SELECT id FROM test_strings WHERE str_value = (SELECT str_value FROM test_strings WHERE str_value ^@ 'ap');
SELECT id FROM test_strings WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM test_strings t2 WHERE t2.str_value ^@ 'g' AND t2.id = test_strings.id);
CREATE TABLE additional_strings (id INTEGER, str_value TEXT);
SELECT a.str_value FROM test_strings a JOIN additional_strings b ON a.str_value ^@ 'a' AND b.str_value ^@ 'grape';
SELECT str_value, (CASE WHEN str_value ^@ 'b' THEN 'Starts with B' ELSE 'Does not start with B' END) AS status FROM test_strings;
SELECT str_value FROM (SELECT str_value FROM test_strings WHERE str_value ^@ 'a') AS subquery WHERE id > 2;
